Okay, so this is the high ticket script. Looks like this. Now there's a lot of steps here. I'm not our salesperson. We did a whole, literally we did a, I don't know, probably 20 hours of training in our high ticket secrets course that goes through this. But this is kind of it. It's a two set script. First is the first person that calls is, what we call our setter. And their job is to find out if the person is going to be a good fit for our coaching program. They basically call a person up, they introduce themselves, they ask them a bunch of questions about them, about their business. They go through the blast, which is kind of, this process kind of show a bunch of stuff. What's happening, what, you know, like we're blasting through a bunch of, what's the right, I can't think of the top, the right word on top of my head. Kind of what's happening and why this is exciting and kind of answering. They've asked a bunch, you've asked a bunch of questions. You're kind of sharing like why, what you have to share and what your coaching program has is going to be awesome. Then you got to posture it, why, why the, why yours is better or different or whatever. Then you probe to find out more about, about their pain, what they're struggling with, the good things, the bad things. You got to find out if they've got credit, they can pay for it. You find out what their goals are and then you share it in the commitments. Okay. So that's the, the, the first person's job, the setters to go through that process with them and find out if they're qualified, if they are qualified at the end of that, then they pass them to the closer. The closer is going to go through the four commitments here. The first one is time. Do you have the time to do it? Number two, are you the decision maker? Can you make decisions? Can you, are you going to be successful with the thing? Number three, do you have the resources you need? Number four is the knowledge. That's kind of what we're going to be doing in the coaching program. Here's what's included and then you sign them up and you close them. So again, there's a lot of psychology behind this and a lot more to do, but if, and so if you want more information on how to do the two step, that's where our high ticket secrets product is. But that's how we sell, that's how we sell our eight, our 15, our 25 and our million dollar packages is through that exact process and it works amazingly well. Right now we're going to shift to the top of the value ladder. I'm going to let Robbie come in and switch microphones. But while he's kind of coming up here, I'm kind of back story. When we first started our call center, we had, we had our, I think at a time, we first came here with like five, maybe 10 guys, four or five people. Robbie came up from Utah and started working with us and helped us take it from four or five people up to 60 people. It just was, was amazing. And then we shut the call center down. We kind of left and I didn't see Robbie for a couple of years. And then when we kind of decided to re bring it back in a different format and a very small format, we built it out and most of you guys met Troy and Kimberly yesterday. They were kind of the initial guinea pigs and they spent a month or so kind of testing things out and trying things. I think in the first, in the first month, I think they made one sale and I was like, man, this sucks. We spent so much energy and effort building out a funnel and driving traffic and everything and just, it wasn't making, it didn't make any money. And so we literally were like a day away from like pulling the plug and I called up Robbie and I was like, Hey man, do you mind like just coming into the office and just like looking to see if they're doing this right? And just kind of, I don't know, just look at it. And like a good salesman's probably like, yeah, I could definitely do that. It's only going to cost you boom and drop some crazy number. I was like, come on, man, we're friends. He's like, yeah, but that's how it works. So I'm like, all right. So I wrote him a check. He came in on a Friday, came on Friday, sat down with Troy and Kimberly spent four hours with him going through the script, going through the process, doing a couple of role plays and then he left. That was Friday. The next morning Troy and Kimberly came in. It was a Saturday. They picked up the phone and within three hour period of time they sold $36,000 worth of coaching. And then I called Robbie back up and he said, man, that was the cheapest money you ever spent. He's like, you should be paying me more for this stuff. And he was right. And then he came back like two weeks later, just more training with them. And literally from that one little four hour session, we were able to take our guys from making almost nothing to averaging $30,000, $40,000 a week. And then after a while, he saw what they were doing and he asked me to come back and we were happy to have him. He's come back and most of you guys probably talked to him at one point, but it's helped take our high end sales process from, I think we'd close $125,000 sell ever. We're trying now averaging about two a week plus a bunch of eight and $15,000 sales as well. It's been blown up. And right now we're doing net sales. We're netting more than we are, more with two people or three people right now than we were with 60 people before. And it's all kind of through this process he's going to be walking us through. He's going to walk you through the two set script that he uses. He's awesome. He's the best sales person I've ever met and excited to have him here. So everyone, let's give a round of applause for Robbie Summers. Wow guys. That's kind of a lot to live up to. I appreciate Russell and I appreciate the opportunity to be here with you guys. There's a couple of things that I really want to express to the people in this room. Most of you I have spoken to on one level or another and most of you on one level or another have trusted me with your time, trusted me with your money. And I want to express my gratitude and my thanks to you because without that we wouldn't be here without you guys doing this and wanting to build and grow your businesses. So I appreciate that and thank you very much. The next thing that I want to do is how many of you guys want to know how to take your $300 buyer, your $10 buyer and turn them into a $25,000 sale? Anybody here interested in that? Okay. Alrighty. Let me just because Russell likes the stick figures, I'm going to just give you guys a quick little demonstration on why and how this works. If I've got my stick figure here and if I asked you guys, if I was to put on my stick figure what part of the body does emotion come from? The heart. Okay. So I've got my heart right here. Okay. And what part of the body does my logic come from? My brain, right? Okay. Where do you guys keep your wallets? Where do you keep them? Your back pocket, side pocket, right? So let's say down here, I got my wallet, right? Which one's closer to my wallet? Is my emotion or my logic closer? Okay. And so the thing that we have to do with this, Russell told me, hey, Roddy, I want you to take your 20 years of experience, your 20 years of knowledge, compress it into 30 minutes and teach these guys everything that you know. And if I could just very, very quickly, the most important thing with doing a high ticket sale is making sure that the people understand the emotion. Now you've got to have the logic there too. You can't have empty air. You can't have an empty product. The script process that we go over, it generally takes about an hour and a half to two hours to go through the whole process. Unless your mic, one tempo, it took about 20 minutes. But that was because that, Russell set that one up, okay? But the whole script process, within about the first five to 15 minutes of every single person that I've talked to in this room, five to 15 minutes, I know and understand that you guys were going to be sitting in this room, that you guys are going to be a buyer, but at the end of our conversation that we're going to have a purchase, we're going to have a sale. And the reason is because of the emotion that we draw out of people. And the reason that we can draw the emotion out is because Russell's got the right funnel done, it's set up, but I could take all the time in the world and I could go through everything with the set script, everything with the closed script, and you guys all have access to that in the black box coaching area. It's all there. The very, very most important thing, even when we get to finding credit, I was talking to, what was your name in the back? Yes, I was talking to Natasha on Thursday. Kimberly and I were talking to her and Natasha said, you ask people how much they have available on their credit cards? Are you serious? I would never, ever do that. And the reason that you can find those questions out, the reason that you can get those things answered is because the emotion is built with the people that we're talking to. Okay, thank you Bonnie. That's it. Okay. Now let's give Bonnie a round of applause. Okay. There's a couple of things and if I was actually on a call, I would have addressed a couple of other things with Bonnie. The whole purpose, the whole premise of what I wanted to do is I've got to find out what motivates Bonnie and Bonnie took a little bit of time to tell me what motivates Bonnie. I had to go through a couple of things. I had to find out. I had to dig a little bit but until I know emotionally what's going to cause Bonnie, Bonnie wants to learn. She wants to teach other people the things that she's passionate about. Other people, David, where's David? David, there's David. David wants to backpack. He wants to travel the country. Other people want RVs. They want to spend more time with their kids. They want to secure their retirement. They want to do those things. It doesn't matter what I'm selling people once I've created that emotion and I logically bridge that gap to help me to accomplish it. The gap for Bonnie is she knows she's a lot of steps away from being able to be successful with her coaching business. She also knows that Russell is over here and he knows how to market. He knows how to drive and build and grow a business. If I can marry the two, if I can bridge those two things together, all of a sudden I give Bonnie the opportunity to reach her goals, her dreams, her successes, everything that she wants and needs know in life. Bonnie, if you thought there was that much of a chance, just the smallest, slightest chance that I could help you to sit in classrooms, to sit in meetings like this and learn for the rest of your life, would you probably tell me anything that I wanted to know to help you build and grow your business? So instead of this much, you need this much? Okay, well I'll take this much. The process behind this is you have to, number one, you have to have a passion and a love for people. As you guys talk to me and as you guys have seen me, I sat down at lunch with a bunch of you and the most important thing is that you're smiling, that you resonate that emotion with people and as you resonate that emotion with people, guess what happens? They feel it, they get excited, they want to be a part of it and you can do that very quickly, very easily on the telephone with your voice inflections, the ups and the downs, those types of things as that happens is it takes place on the telephone that you have the ability to let people become emotional. When I sit down and I talk with somebody about what would it feel like to you to be able to coach your kid soccer team next year because you're working 80 hours a week, what would it feel like to be able to get your kid a college scholarship? What would that mean for you? How would that make a difference? What would that do for you? What would it feel like to have for the network marketers continual passive residual income coming in month in and month out for the rest of your life? What would happen? What would change? All of a sudden that emotion gets pulled out of people. If it's a doctor, what would happen if you didn't have to spend 80 hours a week at your practice? What would you do with your free time? How would your life change? If you're in financial planning, what would happen if you didn't have to spend all day long every day looking for leads? How would that change your business? How would that be different? Then as you do that and you talk to the people about their businesses, where they're at in life, it gives them the opportunity to see and understand that we have the ability to be able to help them. It doesn't matter what your niche is. It doesn't matter if you're the business backman, entrepreneur. It doesn't matter if you're financial services. It doesn't matter if you're selling dogs or coaching people to be NBA stars. It doesn't matter because everybody has their hopes, their goals, and their dreams. If I sit down and I take the time to really care about the people that I'm talking to and I really want to get to know who they are and how we can help them, all of a sudden, if I'm at this point and there's that much of a belief in who it is that we are, what it is that we're doing, all of a sudden it becomes very, very easy. To take them through the rest of the process. Take some time to build that rapport. Take some time to build that emotion. Once I've got that emotion built, Bonnie's going to go through the rest of the process. Maybe right now at this point, Bonnie's not ready to pull out her credit card and give me $25,000. Right now, Bonnie is emotionally tied into working with Russell Brunson. I brought it back into Russell. I brought it back into whatever your product is. As I bring it back into them, as I tie it back into them, I have the ability to logically explain to you how you're working with me, how you're working with us, what our membership site looks like, how we're going to help you to drive traffic in. It's all about creating that emotion. Once you get to the point that you've got this introduction mastered, okay? Again, it's in your black box coaching. Once you get to the point that you get that emotion out of people, all you need past that is somebody with about a fourth grade reading level. Because all the rest of it, the scripts are there. It's very, very, and you customize it to your product. You customize it to what we find out where people are at, how much money they have, and then we find the best fit to help them to achieve and accomplish their goals. It's very, very simple to do if the process, if the funnel is set up correctly. Are there any questions? Anybody have ... Russell told me I had 30 minutes take 20 years worth of experience and compress it down. If I could give you guys 1.1 piece of advice, get to know the people, find out who they are, create that emotion, and then just back it up with logic. The other thing with your call center, with your high ticket sales, the entrepreneurs, you guys, do not be the guys that are on the phone selling. Worst thing in the world you guys can do. Take that out of your mind, get it out of your heads, find people, and we talk about it in high ticket secrets, it's in the black box coaching, teaches you how to find the right person, how to walk through that process, find people, do not be the person on the other end of the line. You're too emotionally attached to your product, you're too emotionally attached to what's going on, and all of a sudden you start talking about all these things that have all this logic, all these reasons why people need to buy, and all the reasons that people need to buy are down here. That's why people buy to be able to take classes for the rest of their lives. People buy because they want to get out of debt. People buy because they want to secure their retirement. People buy because they don't want to work 80 hours a week. People buy because they want to be absentee owners. That's why people buy, and that's where you create that emotion there. What questions do you guys have in regards to the back end, the funnel, anything, Simon? You know what, Simon, that's a great question. You know, I'm not the expert here. That's my program director's role. What you need to do first of all is you need to qualify through me. If you qualify through me, my director is going to answer all those kind of questions for you. He'll go through all the nuts and bolts. It doesn't matter. I set people, I close people. We'll flip them back and forth all day long. Mike, we have three people that work with us. The whole reason that we do is that, gosh, one of us might be at lunch. We might have the day off. We still need to function. We still need to work together. We have three people in one little office. It allows us, just to give you guys an example, it allows us to be able to get the point where consistently week in, week out that we can bring in $50,000, $60,000, $70,000 in revenue for the business at about a 70%, 75% profit margin for you guys. You guys do the number. Gosh, you guys have the ability to put that in your pocket. That's the whole value behind the high ticket sales is if the funnel is created correctly, it allows you to be able to put that in your pocket. Simon, for me, when I get my leads, I have the application and I don't ever read anybody's application. You guys are laughing. You guys are like, gosh, dang it. I filled up that stupid application and Robbie didn't even look at it. Mark, let me explain to you why. The reason that I don't read the applications is because I don't want to prejudge anybody. If you guys knew how many people that we have brought into our programs that we have worked with, that on their applications, they said they have zero marketing budget, zero. They don't have a budget. That's one of the questions on our application. For a lot of salespeople, what are they going to do with that? Oh, that lead, it's no good. A lot of people don't understand and don't realize that they have the ability to use their credit to build and grow business, especially if you're dealing with people that aren't entrepreneurs, aren't businesspeople. A lot of the spaces a lot of you guys are working in, you're dealing with people that are either bizop or they're not people that are normally accustomed to making large purchases, things like that. A lot of people don't in the back of their mind think, gosh, I could use and I could leverage my credit short term because if you get my kid a scholarship to play basketball, how much money does that save me over four years? Hundreds of thousands, and how much are you asking for my, how much are you asking for your top level? What's your top level? 2K. I am not a mathematician. That seems like a better deal, right? $100,000 worth of student loans or 2K on a credit card? Most people don't understand they don't realize that their credit is a tool that they can utilize. We talk about that in the scripts and you look at that as we go through probe finding credit, we talk about how credit works and how most entrepreneurs, you use an example of a real estate investor, they don't go out and put $200,000 into a home. They use the bank's money. They use it short term. They turn around, they put a renter in it. The renter pays the loan off. They use the bank's money instead of theirs. Your credit is the same thing. Most people don't mentally walk through that process. There's probably a lot of you guys in here that are saying, yeah, duh, Robbie, of course my credit is a leveraging tool. The people that most of you guys are talking to don't understand that, don't realize it. It is something that you have to sit down, you have to explain to them. You've got to tie it back into some of this logic up here. That's where that comes in later on down the road with the script. It takes a minute to be able to do it. I don't read the applications. I think you have some good questions on there. I don't want to say that to offend anybody who took a lot of time to fill out the application. I would much rather take the time to hear you, to talk to you, to develop a relationship with you than read a piece of paper. Because I don't want to prejudge you. I don't want to pre-frame you. I want to know who it is that I'm talking to. The best way for me to do that is to be on the phone, talk to you face to face. That's what allows me to be able to do that. That answer your question? I can come back to it and revisit it, but I don't want to talk to people that filled out an application two weeks ago. If you filled out an application, do you know the very best thing that have happened? If you filled out an application, the next step is call this number. If you want to talk to somebody, if you can't wait for somebody to call you, call this number. That's the next step in our process. I want to be talking to you as quickly as possible. Every single day, I come into the office 8, 8.30 in the morning. The very first thing that I do is I print off all the leads that we have that came in from the applications the day before. The first thing that we do is we jump on those leads. If I let you sit for two weeks without making any contact, without trying, without doing anything at all, that leads become so cold at this point. If I can get you within 24, 48 hours, I have the ability to immediately spark that, hey, what are you looking to do with your kid? Why do you want him to go to a big 10 school? Why do you want this? You start asking them those things, it's going to build that emotion right away. My personal favorite is, are you going to put that on your Visa card? Should start with a four? There's a lot of different things. As we talked about before, we're going to leverage this on a line of credit. Looking at the notes here that Troy has taken on you, it looks like probably your Visa card is going to be the best. It should start with a four. How does your name appear on that? Now all of a sudden, because how many of you have a middle initial on your credit cards? A lot of people. Hey, you know what? I don't know if I have it. Let me pull up my credit card. Great. Now, so I haven't asked them. I haven't asked them what's your credit card number, but you've pulled your credit card out. It's sitting right in front of you. You're reading me your credit card. Do you know what happens at that point? I just become an order taker. Great. What's the expiration date? What's the CVV code? Read me the card number four digits at a time. It's a Visa card. It should start with a four. I'll read them back to you. Just very, very simple, very systematically that allows you to be able to do that. Everybody that comes into our program deals with Russell, Brent, and John. If I've got somebody on the phone that is a complete pain in the neck for me, how pleasant are they going to be for Russell? Is Russell, and we go through some very specific qualifying questions. They've got to have the time. Guys, we don't magically build your stuff out for you. You've got to do the work. You've got to put in the time. You've got to put in the effort. You've got to do the things that we teach and train you how to do. If somebody doesn't have the time to put in and they're adamant about it, everybody has the time. There's other reasons. But if they're adamant about not having time, I'm going to bring somebody aboard the team. If I don't feel like somebody's coachable and teachable in my call with them, and I know in two weeks that person's going to be talking to Russell, Russell doesn't get mad. I've known Russell for eight years. He doesn't get mad. If Russell comes in and says, Robbie, why'd you sell that person? Yeah, how dumb I feel. It doesn't happen. But I've got to make sure that people are coachable, that they're teachable, that they believe in what I'm selling. And all those things, they're all part of the script. It's all in there. You just got to have somebody just read through it. It's all there in the black box coaching. Simon, this is your last one. Because it's my job as the program director as the closer to make sure that I'm bringing the right people aboard the team. When the wrong person comes aboard the team and I set the deal, and Russell comes to me because I oversee the call center, Russell comes to me and he goes, Robbie, why'd you bring this person? Hey, you better go talk to Troy. You better go talk to Kimberly closed it. So it's my job, my role to determine whether or not somebody is qualified for Russell's team, for Russell's program. You have to buy into your salespeople. Have to buy into this as a team. I played a lot of football. I played some semi pro football. And I played D-Line. I wasn't a pretty boy. I'd never fit that role. I didn't throw the ball or catch it. If I missed the guy coming through my gap and my linebacker gets him, he doesn't come up to me and say, Robbie, why do you suck? Why'd you miss that? Okay. I missed it. I got blocked out. So something happened. That did never happen. I get blocked out. But if I would have got blocked out, okay, that all of a sudden, okay, that it's that team dynamic, that team atmosphere, and that's why with your salespeople, you really want them. Russell talked about it. Chet Holmes talks about it. One of the questions that I ask them all the time. It sounds so funny because I feel like I'm a cousin Rico or uncle Rico in Napoleon Dynamite. Hey, what did you do in high school? Okay. And not that I want to relive their high school experience, but I want to make sure that I've got somebody that knows how to work hard, that excelled in a sport that was passionate, that understood how to work on a team. I want that atmosphere. I'm looking for those things with my sales guys every single time. And it doesn't matter if it's football, basketball, chess club, debate team. I don't care what they did. I want something that they excelled in. That they know and understand how to excel, how to do it well, because that gives me that great person to build and grow a team with. Alrighty. They take more than 30 minutes, Russell. Okay. If any of you guys have any other questions, feel free to grab me. I've got to cut out. But guys, it really is, it's a pleasure to be working with you. I'm so excited. And again, thank you for trusting me, trusting our team with your dreams, your goals, your passions, your emotions. We want and will help you to reach them. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys. Thanks guys.
